qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Zip slip (path traversal) in VSIX extension extraction writes files outside extensions directory
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same Zip Slip root cause as the advisory: raw zip entry names from easyzip.ExtractZip reach Local.AddExtension, where filepath.Join is used without a boundary check before writing extracted files.

selected_finding.title: Zip slip (path traversal) in VSIX extension extraction writes files outside extensions directory

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code and root cause, and the copied PoC executed against the affected v2.4.1 release produced an arbitrary file write outside the extension directory.
evidence: Advisory root cause: ExtractZip passes attacker-controlled zf.Name to a callback, and AddExtension joins that name with the extension directory using filepath.Join without checking that the result remains under the base path.
Finding report names storage/easyzip/zip.go:61 and storage/local.go:104-117 and describes the same raw zip-entry-name to filepath.Join write path.

.judge/poc_runs/zip-slip-path-traversal/run.log shows the copied PoC checked out the v2.4.1 target, generated a malicious VSIX, and ran code-marketplace add with /tmp/extensions as the extension directory.

.judge/poc_runs/zip-slip-path-traversal/run.log shows the CLI unpacked evil-publisher.test-extension@1.0.0 to /tmp/extensions/evil-publisher/test-extension/1.0.0, then found /tmp/evohunt-zipslip-proof/pwned.txt outside that directory with marker EVOHUNT_ZIP_SLIP_PROOF_OF_CONCEPT_SUCCESS.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC, pinned to the affected v2.4.1 release, processed a crafted VSIX through code-marketplace add and wrote /tmp/evohunt-zipslip-proof/pwned.txt outside /tmp/extensions with the expected proof marker.
